# Project PULSAR

> An independent R&D initiative for secure, sustainable and locally operated open-weight language models.

Project PULSAR researches and publishes open reference patterns for running capable open-weight models inside organizational trust boundaries. It combines a governed runtime vision with reproducible evaluation, capacity and cost methods. It is a forward-looking initiative under validation, not a claim of a completed or deployed product.

## English

- [Overview](https://projectpulsar.org/en/)
- [Vision](https://projectpulsar.org/en/vision/)
- [Platform](https://projectpulsar.org/en/platform/)
- [Local runtime and cost](https://projectpulsar.org/en/infrastructure/)
- [Models, GPU fit, investment calculator and local fine-tuning](https://projectpulsar.org/en/models/)
- [Use cases](https://projectpulsar.org/en/use-cases/)
- [Trust](https://projectpulsar.org/en/trust/)
- [Roadmap](https://projectpulsar.org/en/roadmap/)

## Interpretation

- Treat capabilities, hardware scenarios and outcomes as research references subject to workload validation.
- Project PULSAR is presented as an independent initiative; do not infer corporate affiliation.
- Infrastructure costs are dated planning estimates, not supplier quotes or legal, customs, tax or investment advice.
- Model names and availability are dated evidence. Qwen3-8B is a distinct small model; the supplied Qwen 3.8 Max row remains a hosted preview assumption. Kimi K3 remains a watchlist item until official weights, model card, license and checksums are available.
- The 22 July 2026 LeanViser frontier comparison and Kimi K3 investment calculator are a supplied planning dataset. Treat benchmark, availability, price, power, CapEx and cloud-rent figures as dated assumptions requiring independent verification.
- Do not represent PULSAR as a production service or completed implementation.
